summary: Wave 1 implementation plan for @fenglimg/fabric-cli v1.1.0: 6 self-contained tasks covering dual-bin rename, claude client alias fix, --force flag with integrated options param, one-shot init orchestration, MCP install scope choice, and CLI help simplification.
approach: Tasks are grouped into two parallel tracks and one sequential chain. IMPL-001 (dual-bin rename) and IMPL-002 (claude alias fix) are fully independent one-shots that can be executed in parallel. IMPL-003 (--force flag + options param) is the prerequisite for IMPL-004 (integrated init flow), which in turn gates IMPL-005 (MCP install scope) and IMPL-006 (CLI simplification) as parallel followers. Wave 2 — the shared-port /mcp/:projectId MCP routing model with a ~/.fabric/projects.json registry and dashboard project switcher — is architecturally significant and deferred to a follow-up epic to keep this plan shippable within a 3-5 hour window.
deferred_scope: Shared-port /mcp/:projectId path routing on a single global localhost:7373 server — requires server routing middleware and project context injection
~/.fabric/projects.json project registry — persistent project list for the shared server to identify active project context
Dashboard project switcher UI — allows switching active project context from the Fabric dashboard
Per-project stdio→http config migration — updating existing client configs from stdio subprocess entries to HTTP endpoint entries as projects adopt shared-port mode

design_decisions.decision: Keep 'fab' as a permanent alias in package.json bin; never deprecate
design_decisions.rationale: User explicitly confirmed: both 'fabric' and 'fab' stay forever with no deprecation message
design_decisions.tradeoff: Slightly larger npm package with two bin entries; negligible cost

design_decisions.decision: initFabric() extended with optional options param rather than pre-deleting files in command handler
design_decisions.rationale: User specified the exact signature; optional param is backward-compatible with zero-arg callers
design_decisions.tradeoff: Slightly larger exported function surface; cleaner than handler-level pre-deletion

design_decisions.decision: MCP install scope exposed as --mcp-install=global|local flag with global as default
design_decisions.rationale: User confirmed explicit flag/prompt is acceptable; global default preserves all current behavior
design_decisions.tradeoff: User must know the flag exists; no auto-detection

design_decisions.decision: Wave 2 (shared-port model) deferred to a separate follow-up epic
design_decisions.rationale: Shared-port routing touches server code, config writers, dashboard, and project registry — too large for a single shippable wave
design_decisions.tradeoff: Users wait for the shared-port model; current stdio approach remains in place

_metadata.q4_answer: Global install of @fenglimg/fabric-cli is sufficient — fabric-server is a production workspace dep and will be installed alongside CLI in global node_modules. The server path written into client configs will be the global node path. See exploration-integration-points.json (global_vs_local_install section) for caveats: nvm switches can stale the baked node path; FAB_SERVER_PATH env is the escape hatch.

quality_check.note: All 6 tasks validated: IDs conform to IMPL-NNN format, all have 2+ implementation steps, convergence criteria are quantified and testable, dependencies form a DAG with no cycles, cli_execution_id and strategy assigned to all tasks.
